key_insight: The paper proposes a DEC-based finite-element-style discretization that convergently approximates Whittle–Matérn random fields on manifolds with a universal, parameter-agnostic GMRF formulation.
engineering_relevance: This enables more flexible and computationally efficient uncertainty modeling on curved domains, with potential benefits for inverse problems, sensing, and surrogate modeling on meshes.
method: Uses discrete exterior calculus on well-centered simplicial complexes to approximate Matérn SPDEs on boundaryless Riemannian manifolds.
Constructs sparse precision and covariance matrices for the full (alpha, kappa) family without tuning to specific parameters.
Derives spectral-function representations on well-connected, volume-concentrated discretizations and proposes a low-rank approximator.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports convergence of the GMRF approximation and theoretical spectral-graph results, but does not mention numerical benchmarks or experimental datasets.
watch_item: The approach appears to rely on well-centered, well-connected, volume-concentrated meshes, so mesh quality may be a practical constraint for robust implementation.
paper_title: Approximating Whittle-Matern Fields over Discretized Manifolds
